title: Earthquake Swarm Near Oil Refinery Cluster Signal
thesis: When a cluster of 5+ small earthquakes (magnitude 3.0-4.5) occurs within 50 km of a major US refinery zone in a single week, it signals potential refinery operational stress or community concern; downstream energy prices rise and energy stocks underperform. Seismic activity near refineries creates operational risk perception and potential supply disruption fears.
data_sources: USGS earthquake activity daily data via earthquake_activity adapter, filtered for Texas Gulf Coast and Southern California refinery zones
signal_logic: If 5+ earthquakes magnitude 3.0-4.5 occur within 50 km of major refinery cluster in a single week, AND XLE volume exceeds 20-day average by 25%
entry_exit: Entry: If 5+ earthquakes magnitude 3.0-4.5 occur within 50 km of major refinery cluster in a single week, AND XLE volume exceeds 20-day average by 25% Exit: After 4 weeks or when earthquake activity returns to background rates (1-2 events per week in zone)
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake activity daily data via earthquake_activity adapter, filtered for Texas Gulf Coast and Southern California refinery zones via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Earthquake swarms near refinery zones occur 1-2 times per quarter; seismic activity is persistent and measurable.

schema.idea: Earthquake Swarm Near Oil Refinery Cluster Signal

data.source: USGS earthquake activity daily data via earthquake_activity adapter, filtered for Texas Gulf Coast and Southern California refinery zones

schema.behavior: When a cluster of 5+ small earthquakes (magnitude 3.0-4.5) occurs within 50 km of a major US refinery zone in a single week, it signals potential refinery operational stress or community concern; downstream energy prices rise and energy stocks underperform.

market_impact.rationale: Seismic activity near refineries creates operational risk perception and potential supply disruption fears.

trade_logic.entry: If 5+ earthquakes magnitude 3.0-4.5 occur within 50 km of major refinery cluster in a single week, AND XLE volume exceeds 20-day average by 25%
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or when earthquake activity returns to background rates (1-2 events per week in zone)

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Earthquake swarms near refinery zones occur 1-2 times per quarter; seismic activity is persistent and measurable.

title: Ev Charger Installation Acceleration Play
thesis: When the weekly rate of new EV charger installations accelerates (week-over-week growth jumps 20%+), it signals municipal/private infrastructure spending surge; this often precedes renewable energy demand and tech equipment procurement. EV charger deployment requires semiconductors, software, and networking gear; acceleration signals tech capex cycle.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap weekly charger count growth rate via openchargemap adapter
signal_logic: If weekly charger installation count growth rate jumps 20%+ above prior week AND cumulative monthly new installations exceed prior 4-week average by 25%
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly charger installation count growth rate jumps 20%+ above prior week AND cumulative monthly new installations exceed prior 4-week average by 25% Exit: After 5 weeks or when growth rate decelerates below 10%
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap weekly charger count growth rate via openchargemap adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Charger deployment is accelerating nationally; municipal funding tranches and private buildouts create monthly spikes in growth rates.

data.source: OpenChargeMap weekly charger count growth rate via openchargemap adapter

schema.behavior: When the weekly rate of new EV charger installations accelerates (week-over-week growth jumps 20%+), it signals municipal/private infrastructure spending surge; this often precedes renewable energy demand and tech equipment procurement.

market_impact.rationale: EV charger deployment requires semiconductors, software, and networking gear; acceleration signals tech capex cycle.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly charger installation count growth rate jumps 20%+ above prior week AND cumulative monthly new installations exceed prior 4-week average by 25%
trade_logic.exit: After 5 weeks or when growth rate decelerates below 10%

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Charger deployment is accelerating nationally; municipal funding tranches and private buildouts create monthly spikes in growth rates.

title: Gold Volatility Vol-of-vol Spike
thesis: When the intraday volatility of gold prices begins spiking (vol-of-vol), it signals macro uncertainty; this often precedes a reallocation into defensive commodities. Tech and growth equities underperform when gold volatility rises, signaling flight-to-safety behavior.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for GLD and USO via price_only adapter
signal_logic: If 5-day rolling standard deviation of GLD daily returns exceeds 2x the 60-day rolling mean, AND GLD closes above its 20-day MA
entry_exit: Entry: If 5-day rolling standard deviation of GLD daily returns exceeds 2x the 60-day rolling mean, AND GLD closes above its 20-day MA Exit: After 7 trading days or when vol-of-vol reverts to mean (5-day std dev falls below 1.5x 60-day mean)
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for GLD and USO via price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volatility spikes in commodities occur multiple times per month; gold vol-of-vol thresholds trigger 2-4 times quarterly.

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily prices for GLD and USO via price_only adapter

schema.behavior: When the intraday volatility of gold prices begins spiking (vol-of-vol), it signals macro uncertainty; this often precedes a reallocation into defensive commodities.

market_impact.rationale: Tech and growth equities underperform when gold volatility rises, signaling flight-to-safety behavior.

trade_logic.entry: If 5-day rolling standard deviation of GLD daily returns exceeds 2x the 60-day rolling mean, AND GLD closes above its 20-day MA
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days or when vol-of-vol reverts to mean (5-day std dev falls below 1.5x 60-day mean)

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volatility spikes in commodities occur multiple times per month; gold vol-of-vol thresholds trigger 2-4 times quarterly.

title: Financial Sector Volatility Crush Trade
thesis: When VIX drops 3+ points in a single day while the financial sector (XLF) simultaneously gaps down, it signals a washout in financial stocks despite falling macro volatility—often creating a mean-reversion bounce within days. Financial stocks are oversold when VIX falls but sector falls harder; mean reversion favors the sector.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLF and VIX via price_only adapter
signal_logic: If VIX falls 3+ points day-over-day AND XLF underperforms SPY by 2%+ on the same day AND XLF closes near its lows
entry_exit: Entry: If VIX falls 3+ points day-over-day AND XLF underperforms SPY by 2%+ on the same day AND XLF closes near its lows Exit: After 5 trading days or once XLF closes 1.5%+ above entry
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLF and VIX via price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: VIX daily moves of 3+ points occur several times per month; financial sector gaps down on these days frequently.

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLF and VIX via price_only adapter

schema.behavior: When VIX drops 3+ points in a single day while the financial sector (XLF) simultaneously gaps down, it signals a washout in financial stocks despite falling macro volatility—often creating a mean-reversion bounce within days.

market_impact.rationale: Financial stocks are oversold when VIX falls but sector falls harder; mean reversion favors the sector.

trade_logic.entry: If VIX falls 3+ points day-over-day AND XLF underperforms SPY by 2%+ on the same day AND XLF closes near its lows
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days or once XLF closes 1.5%+ above entry

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: VIX daily moves of 3+ points occur several times per month; financial sector gaps down on these days frequently.

title: Utility Demand Weather Whipsaw
thesis: Sudden swings in seasonal temperature create rapid demand shocks for electricity and natural gas, forcing utility operators to adjust generation and buying patterns within hours. Utilities rally on unexpected demand spikes that increase margin and utilization rates.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily temperature and heating/cooling degree days via weather_series adapter
signal_logic: If 7-day rolling average temperature change exceeds ±8°F from prior week and XLU volume spikes 30% above 20-day average
entry_exit: Entry: If 7-day rolling average temperature change exceeds ±8°F from prior week and XLU volume spikes 30% above 20-day average Exit: After 8 trading days or when temperature stabilizes within 5°F of seasonal norm
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily temperature and heating/cooling degree days via weather_series adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weather volatility occurs weekly in most regions; temperature swings of 8+ degrees happen multiple times per season.

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature and heating/cooling degree days via weather_series adapter

schema.behavior: Sudden swings in seasonal temperature create rapid demand shocks for electricity and natural gas, forcing utility operators to adjust generation and buying patterns within hours.

market_impact.rationale: Utilities rally on unexpected demand spikes that increase margin and utilization rates.

trade_logic.entry: If 7-day rolling average temperature change exceeds ±8°F from prior week and XLU volume spikes 30% above 20-day average
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days or when temperature stabilizes within 5°F of seasonal norm

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weather volatility occurs weekly in most regions; temperature swings of 8+ degrees happen multiple times per season.

title: Small-cap Growth Momentum Rotation
thesis: When IWM (small-cap) 5-day momentum outpaces QQQ (large-cap growth) by 2% or more, it signals a rotation into lower-liquidity names—often driven by retail attention shifts visible in option flow and social media chatter. Small-cap rotation favors consumer discretionary and beaten-down cyclical names.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for IWM (Russell 2000) and QQQ via price_only adapter
signal_logic: If IWM 5-day return minus QQQ 5-day return exceeds +2% AND IWM volume is 15%+ above 20-day average
entry_exit: Entry: If IWM 5-day return minus QQQ 5-day return exceeds +2% AND IWM volume is 15%+ above 20-day average Exit: After 6 trading days or if relative momentum reverses (IWM underperforms by 1% or more)
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for IWM (Russell 2000) and QQQ via price_only adapter via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Small-cap momentum surges occur 2-3 times per month in typical market conditions.

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily prices for IWM (Russell 2000) and QQQ via price_only adapter

schema.behavior: When IWM (small-cap) 5-day momentum outpaces QQQ (large-cap growth) by 2% or more, it signals a rotation into lower-liquidity names—often driven by retail attention shifts visible in option flow and social media chatter.

market_impact.rationale: Small-cap rotation favors consumer discretionary and beaten-down cyclical names.

trade_logic.entry: If IWM 5-day return minus QQQ 5-day return exceeds +2% AND IWM volume is 15%+ above 20-day average
trade_logic.exit: After 6 trading days or if relative momentum reverses (IWM underperforms by 1% or more)

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Small-cap momentum surges occur 2-3 times per month in typical market conditions.

title: Airline Load Factor Collapse Bearish Play
thesis: When load factors (seats sold / seats available) drop 3+ percentage points week-over-week, airlines cut capacity and raise fares; near-term bookings weaken, signaling demand deterioration. Airline load factor weakness signals recession-like travel demand pullback affecting leisure and hospitality.
data_sources: BTS weekly airline load factor via bts_airline_load_factor adapter
signal_logic: If weekly BTS load factor falls 3+ points vs. prior week and falls below its 8-week rolling average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly BTS load factor falls 3+ points vs. prior week and falls below its 8-week rolling average Exit: After 4 weeks or when load factor recovers 2+ points from entry level
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use BTS weekly airline load factor via bts_airline_load_factor adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Load factor swings of 3+ points occur most months; seasonal dips create reliable signal fire.

schema.idea: Airline Load Factor Collapse Bearish Play

data.source: BTS weekly airline load factor via bts_airline_load_factor adapter

schema.behavior: When load factors (seats sold / seats available) drop 3+ percentage points week-over-week, airlines cut capacity and raise fares; near-term bookings weaken, signaling demand deterioration.

market_impact.rationale: Airline load factor weakness signals recession-like travel demand pullback affecting leisure and hospitality.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly BTS load factor falls 3+ points vs. prior week and falls below its 8-week rolling average
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or when load factor recovers 2+ points from entry level

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Load factor swings of 3+ points occur most months; seasonal dips create reliable signal fire.

title: Freight Rail Carload Collapse Signal
thesis: When weekly carloads (cars loaded with freight) drop 8%+ vs. year-ago week, it signals industrial production weakness; this often precedes equity weakness in materials and industrials. Carload collapse is a leading indicator of manufacturing and construction slowdown.
data_sources: Association of American Railroads weekly carload data via fred_series adapter (AAR feeds FRED)
signal_logic: If weekly carloads fall 8%+ year-over-year AND are below their 13-week rolling average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly carloads fall 8%+ year-over-year AND are below their 13-week rolling average Exit: After 6 weeks or when carloads stabilize above the 13-week average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Association of American Railroads weekly carload data via fred_series adapter (AAR feeds FRED) via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Carload data is weekly and volatile; seasonal dips and cyclical weakness create multiple entry points per quarter.

data.source: Association of American Railroads weekly carload data via fred_series adapter (AAR feeds FRED)

schema.behavior: When weekly carloads (cars loaded with freight) drop 8%+ vs. year-ago week, it signals industrial production weakness; this often precedes equity weakness in materials and industrials.

market_impact.rationale: Carload collapse is a leading indicator of manufacturing and construction slowdown.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly carloads fall 8%+ year-over-year AND are below their 13-week rolling average
trade_logic.exit: After 6 weeks or when carloads stabilize above the 13-week average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Carload data is weekly and volatile; seasonal dips and cyclical weakness create multiple entry points per quarter.

title: Mortgage Refinance Wave Google Trends Surge
thesis: When mortgage refinance searches spike, it signals households are actively managing debt stress; subsequent weeks show either housing demand relief (XLY bullish) or wallet tightening (XLP bullish). Successful refinances free up monthly cash flow for discretionary consumer spending.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'refinance mortgage' via google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly Google Trends interest for 'refinance mortgage' spikes 50%+ above 13-week rolling average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly Google Trends interest for 'refinance mortgage' spikes 50%+ above 13-week rolling average Exit: After 3 weeks or when XLY outperforms SPY by 2% cumulatively
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'refinance mortgage' via google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Refinance search surges occur 2-3 times per quarter when rate expectations shift.

schema.idea: Mortgage Refinance Wave Google Trends Surge

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'refinance mortgage' via google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: When mortgage refinance searches spike, it signals households are actively managing debt stress; subsequent weeks show either housing demand relief (XLY bullish) or wallet tightening (XLP bullish).

market_impact.rationale: Successful refinances free up monthly cash flow for discretionary consumer spending.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly Google Trends interest for 'refinance mortgage' spikes 50%+ above 13-week rolling average
trade_logic.exit: After 3 weeks or when XLY outperforms SPY by 2% cumulatively

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Refinance search surges occur 2-3 times per quarter when rate expectations shift.

title: Warehouse Worker Job Posting Surge
thesis: Sudden spikes in warehouse job postings signal supply chain operators are ramping hiring ahead of expected demand; this precedes retail sales strength by 3-6 weeks. Warehouse hiring surge indicates businesses expect strong consumer demand in coming weeks.
data_sources: Indeed.com job posting weekly counts for 'warehouse worker' via html_table scrape adapter
signal_logic: If weekly warehouse worker postings spike 40%+ above 8-week rolling average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly warehouse worker postings spike 40%+ above 8-week rolling average Exit: After 5 weeks or when postings revert to mean
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Indeed.com job posting weekly counts for 'warehouse worker' via html_table scrape adapter via scrape (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Warehouse hiring surges occur ahead of peak seasons (holiday, summer) and demand shocks; typically 2-3 spikes per year, each lasting 4-6 weeks.

data.source: Indeed.com job posting weekly counts for 'warehouse worker' via html_table scrape adapter

schema.behavior: Sudden spikes in warehouse job postings signal supply chain operators are ramping hiring ahead of expected demand; this precedes retail sales strength by 3-6 weeks.

market_impact.rationale: Warehouse hiring surge indicates businesses expect strong consumer demand in coming weeks.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly warehouse worker postings spike 40%+ above 8-week rolling average
trade_logic.exit: After 5 weeks or when postings revert to mean

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Warehouse hiring surges occur ahead of peak seasons (holiday, summer) and demand shocks; typically 2-3 spikes per year, each lasting 4-6 weeks.
